author: kai
description: Resolution Algorithm (RA-1) created. Replaces patchwork fixing with closed-loop: recall → identify → root cause → tasks → execute → verify → simulate → report → memory → close. Mandatory for all agents. Self-evolving via process improvements in every resolution report.
changes: Added recall to Kai startup sequence
Added auto-remediation standard: detection without remediation is incomplete

author: kai
description: Two improvements applied from first resolution: (1) STEP 3 pre-check: for every detection task, confirm a remediation task exists. (2) STEP 5 addition: verify system can self-heal without human intervention — session-dependent fixes are incomplete.
changes: RESOLUTION_ALGORITHM.md STEP 5: added self-heal verification requirement

author: kai
description: SELF-EVOLUTION CYCLE expanded from 11 to 12 steps. New step 10: AGENT REFLECTION — 6 mandatory questions every agent asks after every cycle. Questions focus on bottlenecks, symptom-vs-system, artifact liveness, domain growth, knowledge persistence, and reflection quality. Evolution entries (step 11) now MUST include reflection answers, not just metrics. The reflection loop itself evolves — agents add new questions to PLAYBOOK.md 'Reflection Extensions' and propose them for the constitution. This is the shift from agents-as-runners to agents-as-autonomous-domain-experts. Kai's POST-TASK REFLECTION (added same session) mirrors this at the CEO level.
changes: AGENT_ALGORITHMS.md SELF-EVOLUTION CYCLE: added step 10 (AGENT REFLECTION) with 6 sub-questions
AGENT_ALGORITHMS.md SELF-EVOLUTION CYCLE: renumbered steps 10-11 to 11-12
AGENT_ALGORITHMS.md SELF-EVOLUTION CYCLE step 11: evolution entry must include reflection answers
Reflection loop is self-evolving via PLAYBOOK.md extensions
Parallels Kai POST-TASK REFLECTION but framed for agent autonomy
